entries.name: Lago Argentino
entries.desc: The largest freshwater lake in Argentina, in southern Patagonia. Fed by Andean glaciers, its milky blue arms reach the calving front of the Perito Moreno Glacier in Los Glaciares National Park.

photo.alt: Perito Moreno Glacier meeting Lago Argentino
photo.credit: <!-- photo: perito-moreno-glacier-lago-argentino.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Perito Moreno Glacier Patagonia Argentina Luca Galuzzi 2005.JPG" by Luca Galuzzi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Perito_Moreno_Glacier_Patagonia_Argentina_Luca_Galuzzi_2005.JPG -->

entries.name: Nahuel Huapi
entries.desc: A large glacial lake with a branching, fjord-like shape in the Argentine Lake District, at the heart of Nahuel Huapi National Park. The resort town of Bariloche sits on its southern shore.

photo.alt: Nahuel Huapi Lake near Bariloche
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nahuel-huapi-lake-bariloche.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Punto panoramico - Bariloche.JPG" by Caamy.08 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Punto_panoramico_-_Bariloche.JPG -->

entries.name: Hallstätter See
entries.desc: A deep lake in the Salzkammergut lake district, hemmed by steep wooded mountains. The village of Hallstatt on its shore is a UNESCO site linked to some of the oldest salt mining in Europe.

photo.alt: Hallstatt village on Lake Hallstatt
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hallstatt-lake-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Hallstatt, from opposite side of the lake.jpg" by Herzi Pinki · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hallstatt,_from_opposite_side_of_the_lake.jpg -->

entries.name: Emerald Lake
entries.tag: Yoho National Park, British Columbia, Canada
entries.desc: A vivid green glacial lake in Yoho National Park in the Canadian Rockies, colored by suspended rock flour. Backed by the President Range, it is a popular canoeing and hiking base.

photo.alt: Emerald Lake in Yoho National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: emerald-lake-yoho.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Emerald Lake, Yoho National Park.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Emerald_Lake,_Yoho_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Louise
entries.desc: A turquoise glacial lake in Banff National Park, overlooked by a grand lakeside hotel and the Victoria Glacier at its far end. A hub for canoeing, hiking, and winter ice skating.

photo.alt: Lake Louise's turquoise water framed by glaciers and peaks in Banff National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-louise-banff.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lake Louis, Banff National Park, Canadian Rockies.jpg" by Preeteesh · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Louis,_Banff_National_Park,_Canadian_Rockies.jpg -->

entries.name: Maligne Lake
entries.desc: The largest natural lake in Jasper National Park and the largest glacier-fed lake in the Canadian Rockies. Boat tours reach Spirit Island, a small tree-covered islet framed by surrounding peaks.

photo.alt: Maligne Lake at sunrise, Jasper National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: maligne-lake-jasper.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Sunrise at Maligne lake 3.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sunrise_at_Maligne_lake_3.jpg -->

meta: Largest Lake in Jasper National Park
Largest Glacier-Fed Lake in the Canadian Rockies

entries.name: Moraine Lake
entries.desc: A glacially fed lake in Banff National Park set in the Valley of the Ten Peaks, its intense blue coming from suspended rock flour. The rockpile viewpoint yields one of the Rockies’ most iconic vistas.

photo.alt: Moraine Lake in the Valley of the Ten Peaks
photo.credit: <!-- photo: moraine-lake-banff.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Moraine Lake.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Moraine_Lake.jpg -->

entries.name: Peyto Lake
entries.desc: A glacier-fed lake along the Icefields Parkway in Banff National Park, known for its striking wolf-head shape and vivid blue color when meltwater peaks in summer. Viewed from an easy overlook above.

photo.alt: Peyto Lake viewed from the Bow Summit overlook
photo.credit: <!-- photo: peyto-lake-bow-summit.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Peyto Lake View from Bow Summit.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Peyto_Lake_View_from_Bow_Summit.jpg -->

entries.name: Lago General Carrera / Buenos Aires
entries.desc: A large, deep glacial lake shared by Chile and Argentina in Patagonia. On its Chilean shore, water and wind have carved the Marble Caves, swirling formations reflected in turquoise water.

photo.alt: Swirling turquoise Marble Caves on Lago General Carrera, Patagonia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: marble-caves.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Cuevas de Mármol - Marble Caves, Blue Water, Patagonia, Chile.jpg" by Wescottm · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cuevas_de_M%C3%A1rmol_-_Marble_Caves,_Blue_Water,_Patagonia,_Chile.jpg -->

entries.name: Lago Todos los Santos
entries.desc: A deep emerald lake in Chile’s northern Patagonia, surrounded by dense forest and dominated by the cone of Osorno Volcano. It sits within Vicente Pérez Rosales National Park.

photo.alt: Osorno Volcano rising above emerald Lago Todos los Santos, Chile
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lago-todos-los-santos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Volcán Osorno y Lago todos los santos.jpg" by Andy Heidinger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Volc%C3%A1n_Osorno_y_Lago_todos_los_santos.jpg -->

entries.name: Plitvice Lakes
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed national park where a chain of terraced lakes descends through travertine barriers, linked by cascades and waterfalls. Wooden boardwalks thread across the water through dense forest.

photo.alt: Terraced waterfalls and turquoise pools at Plitvice Lakes National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: plitvice-lakes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Waterfall on a sunny day in Plitvice Lakes National Park.jpg" by Zysko serhii · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Waterfall_on_a_sunny_day_in_Plitvice_Lakes_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Saimaa
entries.desc: The largest lake in Finland and among the biggest in Europe, a maze of interconnected basins, bays, and thousands of islands. Home to the endangered Saimaa ringed seal.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Lake Saimaa's maze of islands and basins, Finland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-saimaa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lake Saimaa aerial.jpg" by kallerna · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Saimaa_aerial.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Annecy
entries.desc: An alpine lake in the French Savoy region, famed for exceptionally clear water thanks to strict conservation. The medieval town of Annecy sits at its northern tip, laced with canals.

photo.alt: Clear alpine waters of Lake Annecy with the Savoy mountains behind
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-annecy.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Lake Annecy @ Saint-Jorioz.jpg" by Guilhem Vellut · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Annecy_@_Saint-Jorioz_(50487088833).jpg -->

entries.name: Königssee
entries.desc: A steep-walled alpine lake in Berchtesgaden National Park in the Bavarian Alps, reputed to be among the cleanest in Germany. Electric boats reach the pilgrimage chapel of St. Bartholomew on its shore.

photo.alt: Königssee alpine lake with the St. Bartholomä pilgrimage chapel on its shore, Berchtesgaden, Germany
photo.credit: <!-- photo: konigssee.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "St. Bartholomä (Königssee).jpg" by Bual.sukhjot1 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:St._Bartholom%C3%A4_(K%C3%B6nigssee).jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Constance (Bodensee)
entries.desc: A large lake on the Rhine at the northern foot of the Alps, bordered by three countries. It supplies drinking water to millions and is edged with vineyards, orchards, and the flowery island of Mainau.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the flowery island of Mainau on Lake Constance
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mainau-island-bodensee.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Aus dem Zeppelin fotografiert, Insel Mainau, Bodensee. 09.jpg" by Holger Uwe Schmitt · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aus_dem_Zeppelin_fotografiert,_Insel_Mainau,_Bodensee._09.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Atitlán
entries.desc: A deep lake filling a volcanic caldera in the Guatemalan highlands, ringed by three volcanoes and Maya villages. Reached mainly by boat, it is among the deepest lakes in Central America.

photo.alt: Lake Atitlán with Volcán Tolimán and Volcán Atitlán rising from the shore, Guatemala
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-atitlan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Lake Atitlan, Volcan Tolimán and Volcan Atitlán.jpg" by Arian Zwegers · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Atitlan,_Volcan_Tolim%C3%A1n_and_Volcan_Atitl%C3%A1n.jpg -->

entries.name: Jökulsárlón Glacier Lagoon
entries.desc: A glacial lagoon at the edge of Vatnaجökull, Iceland’s largest ice cap, where icebergs calve from a retreating glacier tongue and drift toward the sea. Now the deepest lake in Iceland.

photo.alt: Icebergs floating in Jökulsárlón glacier lagoon at the edge of Vatnajökull, Iceland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jokulsarlon-glacier-lagoon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Jökulsárlón April 07-3.JPG" by Martin Peeks · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:J%C3%B6kuls%C3%A1rl%C3%B3n_April_07-3.JPG -->

entries.name: Dal Lake
entries.desc: An urban lake in Srinagar in the Kashmir Valley, known for its wooden houseboats, floating vegetable gardens, and shikara boats. Backed by mountains and Mughal-era gardens along its shore.

photo.alt: Houseboats on Dal Lake in Srinagar, Kashmir
photo.credit: <!-- photo: dal-lake-srinagar.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Houseboat- Dal Lake, srinagar Kashmir.JPG" by Basharat Shah · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Houseboat-_Dal_Lake,_srinagar_Kashmir.JPG -->

entries.name: Pangong Tso
entries.desc: A long, high-altitude endorheic lake in the Himalaya, stretching from Ladakh into Tibet. Despite its brackish water it freezes solid in winter, and its color shifts across shades of blue and green.

photo.alt: Pangong Tso lake in the Himalaya, Ladakh
photo.credit: <!-- photo: pangong-tso-ladakh.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lake Pangong (Pangong Tso) in Ladakh.jpg" by ANKITNARANG297 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Pangong_(Pangong_Tso)_in_Ladakh.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Toba
entries.desc: A vast crater lake in North Sumatra, formed by a supervolcanic eruption and one of the largest volcanic lakes on Earth. The large island of Samosir, home to the Batak people, sits at its center.

photo.alt: Lake Toba volcanic crater lake with Samosir Island, North Sumatra
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-toba-samosir-island.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Toba Lake and Samosir Island.jpg" by Nirwanto Honsono · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Toba_Lake_and_Samosir_Island.jpg -->

meta: One of Largest Volcanic Lakes on Earth

entries.name: Dead Sea
entries.desc: A hypersaline lake bordered by Israel and Jordan, lying at the lowest land elevation on Earth. Its extreme salinity lets bathers float easily, and its mineral mud is used in cosmetics.

photo.alt: Dead Sea shoreline between Israel and Jordan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: dead-sea.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Dead Sea - Jordan Side 04.jpg" by Argenberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dead_Sea_-_Jordan_Side_04.jpg -->

entries.name: Lago di Braies (Pragser Wildsee)
entries.desc: A small emerald lake in the Dolomites of South Tyrol, ringed by sheer rock walls and pine forest. Its wooden rowboats and mountain backdrop make it one of the most photographed spots in the Alps.

photo.alt: Lago di Braies emerald lake in the Dolomites, South Tyrol
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lago-di-braies.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Lago di Braies in agosto 01.JPG" by SimonePascuzzi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lago_di_Braies_in_agosto_01.JPG -->

entries.name: Lake Como
entries.desc: A deep, Y-shaped glacial lake in the Lombardy foothills of the Alps, lined with historic villas and gardens. One of the deepest lakes in Europe and a long-established resort destination.

photo.alt: Lake Como shoreline with villas near Bellagio, Lombardy
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-como-bellagio.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Lac de Côme - Bellagio.jpg" by Jean-Christophe BENOIST · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lac_de_C%C3%B4me_-_Bellagio.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Garda
entries.desc: The largest lake in Italy, straddling three regions between the Alps and the Po plain. Its mild, almost Mediterranean climate supports olive and lemon groves, and stiff northern winds make it a windsurfing hub.

photo.alt: Lake Garda seen from Monte Baldo, northern Italy
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-garda.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Monte Baldo, view across the Lake Garda.JPG" by Dguendel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monte_Baldo,_view_across_the_Lake_Garda.JPG -->

entries.name: Lake Maggiore
entries.desc: A long alpine lake straddling Italy and Switzerland, its northern end reaching into the mountains. Known for the Borromean Islands off Stresa, with their baroque palaces and terraced gardens.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Lake Maggiore and the Borromean Islands, Italy
photo.credit: <!-- photo: borromean-gulf.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "GolfoBorromeoFeb202026.jpg" by Hagai Agmon-Snir · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:GolfoBorromeoFeb202026.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Kawaguchi
entries.desc: One of the Fuji Five Lakes at the northern base of Mount Fuji, popular for its mirror reflection of the peak on calm mornings. A year-round resort area with hot springs and lakeside views.

photo.alt: Mount Fuji reflected in Lake Kawaguchi, Japan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-fuji-lake-kawaguchi.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Mount Fuji from Lake Kawaguchi.jpg" by Subramaniam K V · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mount_Fuji_from_Lake_Kawaguchi.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Nakuru
entries.desc: A shallow alkaline lake in Kenya’s Rift Valley, protected as a national park. Its algae-rich waters draw large numbers of flamingos, and the surrounding reserve shelters rhinos and other wildlife.

photo.alt: Flamingos on Lake Nakuru, Kenya
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-nakuru-flamingos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Flamingos, Lake Nakuru.jpg" by Paul Mannix · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Flamingos,_Lake_Nakuru.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Issyk-Kul
entries.desc: A large, deep endorheic lake in the Tian Shan mountains of Kyrgyzstan. Slightly saline, it never freezes despite the altitude, and its shores draw summer visitors beneath surrounding snow peaks.

photo.alt: Lake Issyk-Kul with the Tian Shan mountains, Kyrgyzstan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-issyk-kul.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Lake Issyk-Kul, Kyrgyzstan.jpg" by Bgag · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Issyk-Kul,_Kyrgyzstan.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Malawi
entries.desc: A long, deep rift-valley lake in southeast Africa, one of the largest and deepest on the continent. It holds hundreds of endemic cichlid fish species, more than any other lake on Earth.

photo.alt: Sunset over the rocky shore of Lake Malawi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-malawi-shore.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Lake Malawi (2489696873).jpg" by Joachim Huber · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Malawi_(2489696873).jpg -->

entries.name: Bacalar Lagoon (Laguna de los Siete Colores)
entries.desc: A long freshwater lagoon in Mexico’s Yucatán, nicknamed the Lagoon of Seven Colors for its bands of blue over a white limestone bed. Home to ancient stromatolite formations.

photo.alt: Bacalar Lagoon's blue waters, Quintana Roo, Mexico
photo.credit: <!-- photo: laguna-de-bacalar.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Laguna de Bacalar.JPG" by Wasquewhat · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Laguna_de_Bacalar.JPG -->

entries.name: Khövsgöl Nuur
entries.desc: A deep, clear lake in northern Mongolia near the Russian border, holding the largest volume of fresh water in the country. Ringed by forest and mountains, it freezes thickly each winter.

photo.alt: View across Khövsgöl Nuur toward the Munku-Sardyk mountain range, Mongolia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-khovsgol-munku-sardyk.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "View from Hovsgol Lake to Munku-Sardyk - panoramio.jpg" by Michael Karavanov · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_from_Hovsgol_Lake_to_Munku-Sardyk_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Inle Lake
entries.desc: A shallow highland lake in Myanmar’s Shan State, known for Intha fishermen who row with one leg and for floating gardens and stilt villages built over the water.

photo.alt: Intha fisherman leg-rowing on Inle Lake, Myanmar
photo.credit: <!-- photo: inle-lake-fisherman.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "FIsherman at Inle Lake.JPG" by mydaydream89 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FIsherman_at_Inle_Lake.JPG -->

entries.name: Phewa Lake
entries.desc: A freshwater lake beside the city of Pokhara in central Nepal, reflecting the Annapurna range on clear days. The Tal Barahi temple stands on a small island near its shore.

photo.alt: Phewa Lake with the Annapurna range reflected on the water, near Pokhara, Nepal
photo.credit: <!-- photo: phewa-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Phewa Lake, Nepal.JPG" by Unish Sitaula · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Phewa_Lake,_Nepal.JPG -->

entries.name: Lake Tekapo
entries.desc: A turquoise glacial lake in New Zealand’s Mackenzie Basin, its color from suspended rock flour. Part of an International Dark Sky Reserve, with the small lakeside Church of the Good Shepherd.

photo.alt: Turquoise waters of Lake Tekapo with the Church of the Good Shepherd on the shore, New Zealand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-tekapo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lake Tekapo 01.jpg" by Krzysztof Golik · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Tekapo_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Wanaka
entries.desc: A large glacial lake in New Zealand’s Southern Alps, gateway to Mount Aspiring National Park. The lone tree standing in its shallows, “That Wanaka Tree,” is a widely photographed landmark.

photo.alt: The lone willow known as "That Wanaka Tree" standing in the shallows of Lake Wanaka, New Zealand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-wanaka-tree.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Lonely tree of Wanaka.jpg" by Tom Hall · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lonely_tree_of_Wanaka.jpg -->

entries.name: Tongariro Emerald Lakes
entries.desc: Small crater lakes along the Tongariro Alpine Crossing, colored bright green by dissolved volcanic minerals. They sit within a dual UNESCO World Heritage park of active volcanoes.

photo.alt: The bright green Emerald Lakes along the Tongariro Alpine Crossing, New Zealand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tongariro-emerald-lakes.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Emerald lakes on Tongariro Alpine Crossing.jpg" by Markrosenrosen · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Emerald_lakes_on_Tongariro_Alpine_Crossing.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Ohrid
entries.desc: One of Europe’s oldest and deepest lakes, shared by North Macedonia and Albania. Its ancient isolation supports many endemic species, and the lakeside town of Ohrid is a UNESCO site.

photo.alt: Lake Ohrid on the border of North Macedonia and Albania
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-ohrid.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Ohrid Lake.jpg" by Amazone7 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ohrid_Lake.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Titicaca
entries.desc: The largest lake in South America by volume and the highest large navigable lake in the world, shared by Peru and Bolivia in the Andes. Known for the Uros people’s reed islands.

photo.alt: Lake Titicaca in the Andes, on the border of Peru and Bolivia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-titicaca.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Lake Titicaca - Road to Bolivia (8385839315).jpg" by Alex Proimos · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Titicaca_-_Road_to_Bolivia_(8385839315).jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Baikal
entries.desc: The world’s deepest and oldest freshwater lake, in southern Siberia, holding roughly a fifth of the planet’s unfrozen fresh water. Rich in endemic species and famed for clear winter ice.

photo.alt: Lake Baikal's clear winter ice in southern Siberia, Russia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-baikal-winter.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lake Baikal in winter.jpg" by Sergey Pesterev · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lake_Baikal_in_winter.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Kivu
entries.desc: A deep rift-valley lake on the border of Rwanda and the DR Congo, ringed by terraced hills. One of a few lakes that hold large dissolved volumes of methane and carbon dioxide at depth.

photo.alt: Lake Kivu ringed by terraced hills, on the border of Rwanda and the Democratic Republic of Congo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-kivu.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "View over Lake Kivu from Home St. Jean - Karongi-Kibuye - Western Rwanda.jpg" by Adam Jones, Ph.D. · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_over_Lake_Kivu_from_Home_St._Jean_-_Karongi-Kibuye_-_Western_Rwanda.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Bled
entries.desc: A small glacial lake in the Julian Alps, ringed by mountains and known for the tiny island at its center, topped by a pilgrimage church, and a cliff-top medieval castle.

photo.alt: Lake Bled with its island church and cliff-top castle, seen from the Mala Osojnica viewpoint
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-bled-osojnica-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "A panoramic view of Lake Bled from the mala Osojnica area above" by Meiko21 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_panoramic_view_of_Lake_Bled_from_the_mala_Osojnica_area_above.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Bohinj
entries.desc: The largest permanent lake in Slovenia, set within Triglav National Park in the Julian Alps. Quieter and wilder than nearby Bled, it is fed by alpine streams and popular for hiking and swimming.

photo.alt: Lake Bohinj ringed by the peaks of Triglav National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-bohinj.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Autumn at Bohinj lake" by Dreamy Pixel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Autumn_at_Bohinj_lake.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Brienz
entries.desc: A vivid turquoise lake east of Interlaken, colored by glacial meltwater and rock flour. The Giessbach Falls tumble down its southern shore, reached by a historic funicular.

photo.alt: Turquoise waters of Lake Brienz from a shoreline viewpoint near Bönigen
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-brienz.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "CH.BE.Boenigen Brienzersee-Viewpoint 2021-08-13 8237 16x9-R 8K" by Roy Egloff · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:CH.BE.Boenigen_Brienzersee-Viewpoint_2021-08-13_8237_16x9-R_8K.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Geneva (Lac Léman)
entries.desc: A large crescent-shaped lake on the course of the Rhône, shared by Switzerland and France. Its shores hold Geneva, Lausanne, and the terraced Lavaux vineyards, with the Alps rising to the south.

photo.alt: Lake Geneva viewed from the Lavaux vineyard terraces, Switzerland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-geneva-lavaux-vineyards.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "20201009 view on Lake Geneva from the Lavaux.jpg" by Benoit Pittet · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:20201009_view_on_Lake_Geneva_from_the_Lavaux.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Lucerne (Vierwaldstättersee)
entries.desc: A branching, fjord-like lake in central Switzerland surrounded by steep peaks such as Pilatus and Rigi. Tied to the founding legends of the Swiss Confederation and served by historic paddle steamers.

photo.alt: Lake Lucerne with Mount Pilatus rising above the water, Switzerland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-lucerne-pilatus.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Luzern Pilatus and Lake 1170975.jpg" by Ermell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Luzern_Pilatus_and_Lake_1170975.jpg -->

meta: Lake of the Four Forest Cantons

entries.name: Lake Thun
entries.desc: A turquoise glacier-fed lake in the Bernese Oberland, flanked by the town of Thun and the Niesen pyramid peak. It forms the western half of the twin lakes around Interlaken.

photo.alt: Lake Thun with the Niesen pyramid peak above Merligen
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-thun-niesen.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Mountain Niesen - View from Merligen Thunersee" by Marco Ghinolfi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mountain_Niesen_-_View_from_Merligen_Thunersee.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Zürich
entries.desc: A curving lowland lake stretching southeast from the city of Zürich, framed by rolling hills and vineyards. Popular for lakeside swimming areas, promenades, and steamer cruises.

photo.alt: Panoramic view of the city of Zürich and Lake Zürich from Waidberg, Switzerland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-zurich-city-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Panoramic view from Waidberg of the city of Zurich and Lake Zurich, 2010.jpg" by Vadimlucky · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Panoramic_view_from_Waidberg_of_the_city_of_Zurich_and_Lake_Zurich,_2010.jpg -->

entries.name: Oeschinen Lake
entries.desc: A high mountain lake above Kandersteg in the Bernese Oberland, cradled by peaks of the Blüemlisalp. Reached by gondola and a short hike, it is fed by surrounding glaciers.

photo.alt: Oeschinen Lake beneath the peaks of the Blüemlisalp above Kandersteg
photo.credit: <!-- photo: oeschinen-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Oeschinen Lake in the Bernese Oberland" by Yuuuuk 10 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Oeschinen_Lake_in_the_Bernese_Oberland.jpg -->

entries.name: Iskanderkul
entries.desc: A high mountain lake in the Fann Mountains of Tajikistan, dammed long ago by a landslide. Its turquoise water sits amid rugged peaks, a popular trekking destination in the region.

photo.alt: Turquoise Iskanderkul lake amid the Fann Mountains, Tajikistan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: iskanderkul-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Tajikistan. Iskander-Kul" by Dan Lundberg · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tajikistan._Iskander-Kul.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Victoria
entries.desc: The largest lake in Africa and the world’s largest tropical lake, shared by three countries. A source of the Nile, it supports major fisheries and dense populations along its shores.

photo.alt: Shoreline of Lake Victoria, Africa's largest lake
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-victoria.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Africa Lake Victoria 10 006" by Michael Shade · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Africa_Lake_Victoria_10_006.jpg -->

entries.name: Loch Lomond
entries.desc: The largest lake by surface area in Great Britain, at the heart of Loch Lomond and the Trossachs National Park. Dotted with wooded islands, it marks the edge of the Scottish Highlands.

photo.alt: Loch Lomond framed by wooded hills in the Trossachs
photo.credit: <!-- photo: loch-lomond.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Loch Lomond Panoramam" by Davide Giuliani · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Loch_Lomond_Panoramam.JPG -->

entries.name: Loch Ness
entries.desc: A long, deep loch along the Great Glen fault in the Scottish Highlands, holding more fresh water than all English and Welsh lakes combined. Famous for the folklore of its legendary monster.

photo.alt: Urquhart Castle ruins on the shore of Loch Ness
photo.credit: <!-- photo: loch-ness-urquhart-castle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Urquhart Castle, Loch Ness, Scotland" by DraganaF · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Urquhart_Castle,_Loch_Ness,_Scotland.jpg -->

meta: Largest Water Volume in the UK

entries.name: Crater Lake
entries.desc: The deepest lake in the United States, filling the collapsed caldera of Mount Mazama in the Oregon Cascades. Fed only by rain and snow, it is prized for its intense blue clarity.

photo.alt: Deep blue water of Crater Lake filling the Mount Mazama caldera
photo.credit: <!-- photo: crater-lake-oregon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Crater Lake National Park Oregon" by Jeffrey Johnson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Crater_Lake_National_Park_Oregon.jpg -->

entries.name: Flathead Lake
entries.desc: The largest natural freshwater lake in the western United States, in northwest Montana. A remnant of a vast glacial lake, its clear waters and surrounding orchards draw summer recreation.

photo.alt: Flathead Lake in northwest Montana
photo.credit: <!-- photo: flathead-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "View of Flathead Lake" by William Neuheisel · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_of_Flathead_Lake_(14540687703).jpg -->

meta: Largest Natural Lake in the Western US

entries.name: Jenny Lake
entries.desc: A glacial lake at the foot of the Teton Range in Grand Teton National Park, formed by glaciers that carved the surrounding canyons. A trailhead and shuttle boat access hikes into the peaks.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Jenny Lake below the Teton Range
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jenny-lake-grand-teton.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Aerial image of Jenny Lake (view from the east)" by Carsten Steger · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aerial_image_of_Jenny_Lake_(view_from_the_east).jpg -->

entries.name: Lake McDonald
entries.tag: Glacier National Park, Montana, United States
entries.desc: The largest lake in Glacier National Park, carved by glaciers and lined with multicolored pebbles visible through its clear water. The Going-to-the-Sun Road runs along its shore.

photo.alt: Sunrise over Lake McDonald in Glacier National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-mcdonald-glacier-np.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Sunrise over Lake McDonald - Glacier National Park" by aparlette · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sunrise_over_Lake_McDonald_-_Glacier_National_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Powell
entries.desc: A large reservoir on the Colorado River spanning Utah and Arizona, formed by Glen Canyon Dam. Its labyrinth of red-rock canyons and side channels makes it a major houseboating destination.

photo.alt: Red-rock canyon channel of Lake Powell, Utah
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-powell-cathedral-canyon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Cathedral Canyon, Lake Powell Near Rainbow Bridge National Monument, Utah" by Ken Lund · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cathedral_Canyon,_Lake_Powell_Near_Rainbow_Bridge_National_Monument,_Utah_(14201214732).jpg -->

entries.name: Lake Tahoe
entries.desc: A large, deep alpine lake straddling California and Nevada in the Sierra Nevada, ringed by forested peaks and ski resorts. Noted for its clarity and its year-round mix of skiing and beach recreation.

photo.alt: Blue waters of Lake Tahoe, Sierra Nevada
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lake-tahoe-blue-water.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Blue water, Lake Tahoe" by Downtowngal · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blue_water,_Lake_Tahoe.jpg -->

entries.name: Ha Long Bay (Freshwater: Ba Bể Lake)
entries.desc: The largest natural freshwater lake in Vietnam, set within Ba Bể National Park among karst limestone peaks and forest. Its name means Three Bays, and boat trips pass caves and waterfalls.

photo.alt: Morning mist over Ba Bể Lake, Vietnam
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ba-be-lake-morning-mist.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Babenp 2007 01 3" by Rolf Müller · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Babenp_2007_01_3.jpg -->
